frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: Pupil size as index of subcortical neuromodulatory arousal shaping cortical state.

frameworks.name: aperiodic / 1-f cortical activity
frameworks.one_line: Arousal modulates the aperiodic component of population activity, not only band power.

frameworks.name: homogeneous-modulator account of arousal
frameworks.one_line: Traditional view that arousal uniformly modulates cortex across regions.

core_question: How do spontaneous fluctuations in pupil-linked arousal map onto frequency-resolved neuronal population activity across the human cortex?
key_assumptions: Pupil size indexes central arousal / neuromodulatory state
Spontaneous pupil dilations have a definable peak time anchoring cortical effects
MEG band power reflects local cortical population activity
manipulations: Time-locking cortical activity to peak of spontaneous pupil dilations
Parsing cortical signal into low (2-8 Hz), intermediate (8-32 Hz), and high (>64 Hz) bands
Decomposing population activity into periodic vs aperiodic components
key_findings: Low-frequency (2-8 Hz) power decreases in temporal and lateral frontal cortex around pupil peak
High-frequency (>64 Hz) power increases in mid-frontal regions following the low-frequency drop
Intermediate band (8-32 Hz) in occipito-parietal cortex shows monotonic and inverted-U relations to arousal
Aperiodic component of cortical activity changes widely with pupil-linked arousal
interpretations: Arousal is not a homogeneous cortical modulator but a region- and frequency-specific cascade
Pupil-linked arousal restructures both oscillatory and aperiodic cortical dynamics
